evidences.title: Simple Green/Red Candle Trading Strategy for Cryptocurrency
evidences.content: 2. Enter Trades At each chosen trading time, check the most recently closed H1 or M15 candle: Green Candle (close > open): Place a buy order immediately after the candle closes. Red Candle (close < open): Place a short/sell order immediately after the candle closes. Skip neutral candles (close = open). Example: In Sydney, Australia (AEST, UTC+10), at 14:00 local time, if the H1 candle for BTC/USDT is green, enter a buy. In São Paulo, Brazil (BRT, UTC-3), at 13:00 local time, if the H1 candle for ETH/USDT is red, enter a short. 3. Set Exit Points Stop Loss (SL): [...] Trade 2: ETH/USDT (H1, Afternoon Session) Location: London, UK (12:00 BST) Candle: H1 closes red (-$150) Action: Short at $59,500 SL: $59,650 (high of prior candle, $150 risk) TP: $59,200 (1:2 ratio, $300 profit) Outcome: Price drops to $59,200 for profit; or rises to $59,650, triggering a loss. Why This Strategy Shines Simplicity: No need for complex indicators—just follow candlestick colors. Flexibility: Trade at times that suit your local schedule, aligned with global market sessions. Volatility-Friendly: Perfect for crypto’s 5-10% daily swings, especially on pairs like BTC/USDT. Risk Control: 1% risk per trade a...

evidences.title: BITCOIN Historical Datasets 2018-2026 Binance API - Kaggle
evidences.content: ## Dataset Overview This dataset contains historical price data for Bitcoin (BTC/USDT) from January 1, 2018, to the present. The data is sourced using the Binance API, providing granular candlestick data in four timeframes: 15-minute (15M) 1-hour (1H) 4-hour (4H) 1-day (1D) This dataset includes the following fields for each timeframe: [...] ## Data Sources Binance API: Used for retrieving 15-minute, 1-hour, 4-hour, and 1-day candlestick data from 2018 to the present. ## File Contents 1. btc_15m_data_2018_to_present.csv: 15-minute interval data from 2018 to the present. 2. btc_1h_data_2018_to_present.csv: 1-hour interval data from 2018 to the present. 3. btc_4h_data_2018_to_present.csv: 4-hour interval data from 2018 to the present. 4. btc_1d_data_2018_to_present.csv: 1-day interval data from 2018 to the present. ## Automated Daily Updates This dataset is automatically updated every day using a custom Python program. evidences.title: Simple Green/Red Candle Trading Strategy for Cryptocurrency
evidences.content: 1. Set Up Your Trading Environment Choose a Platform: Use trusted exchanges like Binance, Bybit, or KuCoin with robust charting tools. Select a Pair: Focus on high-liquidity pairs (e.g., BTC/USDT, ETH/USDT) to minimize spreads and slippage. Prepare Capital: Ensure you have enough funds and risk only 1% per trade to protect your account. Access Charts: Set up H1 or M15 charts on TradingView or your exchange’s platform. Pick Trading Times: Choose three daily times in your local time zone that align with global market activity. Examples: + New York, USA (ET, UTC-4): 08:00 (Asia close), 13:00 (Europe/U.S.), 17:00 (U.S.). + London, UK (BST, UTC+1): 07:00 (Asia), 12:00 (Europe/U.S.), 16:00 (U.S.). + Tokyo, Japan (J Graeme, UTC+9): 09:00 (Asia), 14:00 (Europe), 18:00 (U.S.). evidences.title: Bitcoin (BTC) Price USD Today, News, Charts, Market Cap | Coinbase
evidences.content: The remaining ~1 million BTC will be released gradually through mining rewards until around the year 2140. Every four years, a “halving” event cuts the mining reward in half, slowing the issuance of new coins. This predictable supply schedule is one reason many people view Bitcoin as a deflationary asset. Because Bitcoin’s circulating supply changes slowly, changes in market demand are a major factor affecting the BTC/USD price and the Bitcoin price today. ### How long does it take to mine 1 Bitcoin? 󰀎 [...] 󰌾 364.93% ### Sellers 88K 󰌾 418.98% ### Searched 7.3K ## Market Stats ### Market Cap $1.42T 󰌾 1.15% ### FDV $1.49T ### Circ.
